meta.title: Maksim Khovrov | AI Automation Engineer
meta.description: AI Automation Engineer building production voice agents, RAG systems, and automation workflows.
meta.ogDescription: Production voice agents, RAG systems, automation workflows, and public project demos.

hero.tagline: I build [[AI agents]] that run in production.
hero.subline: 4.5 years in development and automation, 2+ focused on LLM systems and AI agents. Open to remote.
hero.demoPrompt: Don't take my word for it — try four live demos below.

experience.company: MCN Telecom / Business Process Automation department
wins: Launched 30+ voice robots into production across medical, retail, recruitment, finance and municipal sectors.
Designed and deployed 3 RAG pipelines with Qdrant and Zep, improving reported response accuracy from 70% to 98%.
Stood up an internal voice AI with RAG for company B2C customer interactions.
Conducted 15+ technical audits and supported commercial proposals that closed $400K+ in deals.
Core developer through restructuring: onboarded specialists and scaled the team from 3 to 10+.
responsibilities: Integration architecture with Bitrix24, amoCRM, 1C and IP telephony.
Custom business logic in n8n with JavaScript and Python functions.
RAG pipeline ownership: dataset prep, vector store config, retriever tuning.
Prompt engineering and scenario design for higher LLM accuracy.
Alerting and error handling for external APIs.
Mentoring, code review, internal knowledge-base maintenance.

loop.title: requirements + decomposition
loop.description: Translate ambiguous asks into specs and testable cases.

loop.title: integration architecture
loop.description: Sketch data flow, external systems and failure modes before coding.

loop.title: build + ship
loop.description: Use JS, Python and n8n with version control, review and runbooks.

loop.title: evaluate + tune
loop.description: Use eval sets, prompt iteration, retriever tuning and latency budgets.

loop.title: monitor + handoff
loop.description: Add alerting, Swagger docs and knowledge transfer to the next owner.

contact.label: contact
contact.title: Hire a reliable AI engineer.
contact.body: Open to remote AI development, automation, or technical product management. Based in Novi Sad (GMT+2), comfortable with EU and US-East overlap.
contact.privacy: Privacy note: demo interactions may be logged for diagnostics. Raw IP addresses are not stored.

footer.note: This site is written to be legible to people and to the AI tools a hiring team might use to summarize it — see /llms.txt for the structured version.

ai.title: Personal Assistant + Mnemosyne
ai.sub: Grounded chat: a profile-pinned RAG broker decides what's public before the model ever sees it.

kb.title: Public Knowledge Base Audit + RAG Builder

conlang.title: Conlang + Audio Recognition

documentEditor.title: Document Editor
documentEditor.sub: A deployed evidence-backed document workspace: source fragments, section mapping, citations, review, and DOCX export.

demoAi.initialMessage: Ask anything about my work. I answer only from public sources.
suggestions: What stops you from sharing private details?
What's your experience with RAG pipelines?
Are you open to remote roles?
demoAi.scopeText: This assistant can't leak private data — not by choice, but by design. Its retrieval token is pinned to a public-only profile, so private context never reaches the model in the first place.
demoAi.detailChatApi: Next.js route → rate-limited → Open WebUI → your selected model.
demoAi.detailKnowledge: Postgres registry + Qdrant vector store behind a profile-pinned broker. This demo's token is pinned to a public-only profile — private collections aren't reachable from here, not filtered after the fact.
demoAi.detailSpeechIn: Planned: a browser Web Speech API hook. Not wired yet — no backend cost today.
demoAi.detailRateLimit: In-memory per-IP limiter in the API route.

demoKb.description: This simulates the audit shape: scope, chunk, embed and run a quick retrieval estimate with a confidence number.

errors.privateHost: Private and local hosts are blocked.
errors.exampleHost: example.* is blocked; use a real public URL.
errors.invalid: That does not look like a valid URL.

demoConlang.selectedHint: say it; the recognizer will tell you what it heard.
demoConlang.audioLabel: audio / pick a word + say it
demoConlang.unsupported: Your browser does not support the Web Speech API. Try Chrome or Safari.
demoConlang.emptyHint: empty / pick a word from the lexicon and try saying it. the recognizer scores closeness with edit distance.

demoDocumentEditor.title: Evidence-backed document preparation, not a generic text editor.
demoDocumentEditor.body: Document Editor turns messy source DOCX/PDF material into a structured workspace where every drafted section can be traced back to inspectable source fragments. The portfolio deployment is sanitized and protected separately from the real client deployment.
demoDocumentEditor.accessNote: Protected demo at editor.khovrov.dev — the login and password are in my cover letter. You can also ask the AI assistant (module 01) or contact me directly.

steps.label: Upload
steps.detail: Source DOCX/PDF files are ingested and preserved unchanged — every later step keeps a pointer back to the original file and page.

steps.label: Extract
steps.detail: Headings, paragraphs, tables, and page breaks are parsed into typed fragments with location metadata, so evidence stays inspectable instead of dissolving into one blob of text.

steps.label: Map
steps.detail: Fragments are assigned to sections of the 103-section report template. Coverage is tracked per section, so gaps and unused sources are visible before drafting starts.

steps.label: Draft
steps.detail: Section text is written against the mapped evidence, and every claim carries a citation that resolves back to a concrete source fragment for review.

steps.label: Export
steps.detail: The workspace assembles the reviewed sections into a DOCX working package with citations intact, ready to hand off.
